steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content: The angles in a triangle sum to $180^\circ$. Since angles $N$ and $P$ each measure $52^\circ$, the remaining angle plus the two known angles equals the triangle total: $$M+52^\circ+52^\circ=180^\circ$$

steps.title: Subtract the known angles
steps.content: Combine the two known angles: $$52+52=104$$ Then subtract from the triangle total: $$180-104=76$$ The answer is 76.
